sections.paragraph: an unused root (cogn. עוּץ, אוּץ), prop. to impress, to immerse, to engrave. Hence עֵט a style. Arabic غاط to impress, to immerse, to imprint, as feet into the sand; also, to dig. II. to swallow down great morsels. VI. to dip one another into the water. غُوطَةُ soft sandy ground, irrigated with water, and planted with trees; comp. غاص to dip oneself under water, to make water. [In Thes. the meanings given here to this root are spoken of very doubtfully, and the word עֵט is derived from the idea of hardness.]
